examples.title: Algorithm: Ethereum Block Structure
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the structure of an Ethereum block
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EthereumBlock
INPUT: parentHash, stateRoot, transactionsRoot, receiptsRoot, timestamp, difficulty, nonce
OUTPUT: block

BEGIN
 block.header.parentHash = parentHash
 block.header.stateRoot = stateRoot
 block.header.transactionsRoot = transactionsRoot
 block.header.receiptsRoot = receiptsRoot
 block.header.timestamp = timestamp
 block.header.difficulty = difficulty
 block.header.nonce = nonce
 block.body.transactions = []
 block.body.ommers = []
 RETURN block
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the components of an Ethereum block, including the header and body.

examples.title: Python: Ethereum Block Creation
examples.description: Python code demonstrating the creation of an Ethereum block
examples.code:
import hashlib
import time

class EthereumBlock:
 def __init__(self, parent_hash, state_root, transactions_root, receipts_root, timestamp, difficulty, nonce):
 self.header = {
 'parent_hash': parent_hash,
 'state_root': state_root,
 'transactions_root': transactions_root,
 'receipts_root': receipts_root,
 'timestamp': timestamp,
 'difficulty': difficulty,
 'nonce': nonce
 }
 self.body = {
 'transactions': [],
 'ommers': []
 }

# Create a new Ethereum block
block = EthereumBlock(
 parent_hash='0x1234567890abcdef',
 state_root='0x1234567890abcdef',
 transactions_root='0x1234567890abcdef',
 receipts_root='0x1234567890abcdef',
 timestamp=int(time.time()),
 difficulty=100,
 nonce=0
)

print(block.header)
print(block.body)
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a class for creating Ethereum blocks and demonstrates how to create a new block.

examples.explanation: This Java code defines a class for verifying Ethereum blocks and demonstrates how to verify a block's state root, transactions root, and receipts root.
